scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within bioinformatics data processing scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Marker-based scoring approach is standard for scRNA annotation; confidence levels and novel cluster flagging are methodologically appropriate; PBMC-only limitation explicitly documented.

code_usability.detail: Script compiles and runs; demo mode functional with built-in marker database; path traversal check documented in SKILL.md.

functional_suitability.note: Marker Database Coverage section documents PBMC-only limitation and tissue routing gap; path traversal check documented in SKILL.md; --demo flag in Quick Check.

reliability.note: Input Validation at Step 1 enforces early scope exit; path traversal check documented for --markers file path.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md 130 lines — lean; no references/ directory for progressive disclosure.

agent_usability.note: Fallback Template present; Input Validation at top; --demo flag in Quick Check; Seurat/Scanpy alternative tool suggestion in refusal message.

human_usability.note: Natural trigger language in description; PBMC limitation clearly documented; Risk Assessment table present.

security.note: Path traversal check documented in SKILL.md for --markers file path; no hardcoded secrets.

maintainability.note: Marker Database Coverage section documents tissue routing gap; extensibility path clear; path traversal check documented but not implemented in script.

agent_specific.note: Input Validation at top enforces early exit; Seurat/Scanpy alternative tool suggestion in refusal; path traversal check documented. Deduction: --top-n not in argparse; tissue routing not implemented.

key_strengths: Input Validation at Step 1 enforces early scope exit before any processing
Marker Database Coverage section explicitly documents PBMC-only limitation and tissue routing gap with extensibility guidance
Path traversal check documented in SKILL.md for --markers file path
Confidence levels (High/Medium/Low) and novel cluster flagging are well-designed output features

recommendations.title: Marker database hardcoded to 6 PBMC immune types only — fourth consecutive round
recommendations.problem: The MARKER_DATABASE contains only 6 immune cell types; --tissue and --species parameters are documented but not wired to tissue-specific or species-specific databases. This gap persists into v4.
recommendations.root_cause: Script was scaffolded with a minimal PBMC demo database without implementing tissue/species routing; not addressed across four rounds.
recommendations.fix: Add tissue-specific marker dictionaries (e.g., liver, lung, brain) and route based on --tissue argument. Alternatively, integrate with CellMarker or PanglaoDB via a reference file.

recommendations.title: Path traversal check documented but not implemented in script
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md documents path traversal check for --markers file path, but the script does not enforce it at runtime.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation fix applied to SKILL.md but not translated into script logic.
recommendations.fix: Add path validation in main.py: if '../' in args.markers or os.path.isabs(args.markers): print('Path traversal detected', file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1).

recommendations.title: --top-n parameter documented in SKILL.md but absent from script argparse
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md documents --top-n parameter (default: 20) for top N markers per cluster but the script does not include it in argparse.
recommendations.root_cause: Script was scaffolded without implementing the top-n parameter.
recommendations.fix: Add --top-n argument to argparse with default 20. Pass to annotate_cluster() as the top_n parameter.
